methods.description: Process for preparing tree ring samples for chemical and isotopic analysis to study the effects of the Parícutin eruption.

steps.description: Several cores from pine trees located in the ash fall zone and in the town of Tancitaro are examined for chemical signatures.
steps.searchable_summary: collect tree core samples from specified locations

steps.description: Samples from individual tree rings were obtained using a Dremel tool and/or razor blade to extract a whole wood sample from each ring.
steps.searchable_summary: extract whole wood samples from tree rings

steps.description: Samples were freeze-dried to a constant weight and then weighed into precleaned, pre-weighed, trace-metal free polypropylene centrifuge tubes.
steps.searchable_summary: freeze-dry and weigh samples into centrifuge tubes

steps.description: For every 25 mg of sample, 1 mL of concentrated nitric acid was added to the tube.
steps.searchable_summary: add nitric acid to samples for digestion

steps.description: Samples were digested at room temperature for three days and then heated to 70 C in an ultrasonic bath for three hours.
steps.searchable_summary: digest samples at room temperature and heat in ultrasonic bath

steps.description: Aliquots were diluted with ultra-pure water and spiked with internal standards (Sc, Nd, In).
steps.searchable_summary: dilute samples and add internal standards

methods.description: Analysis of elemental abundances and stable carbon isotopic measurements to study the chemical signatures in tree rings.

steps.description: Elemental abundances of sulfur and phosphorus were analyzed in solution using a VG Elemental Axiom magnetic sector ICP-MS.
Stable carbon isotopic measurements were performed on a Costech 4010 Elemental Analyzer connected to a Thermo Delta V Advantage continuous flow mass spectrometer.
Data are reported in standard delta notation with an error of ±0.2‰.
steps.searchable_summary: report isotopic data in delta notation
